manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: shawl, butterfly, porch, pepperoni, nightshirt, carton, caribou, walrus, kleenex.
Your opponent's team words are: flute, beefburger, chopstick, warthog, owl, lamp, popcorn, firetruck.
Distractor words are: ball, matchstick, photo, lamb, bed, nectarine, pitchfork.
The kill words are: bluebird.

Looking at broader patterns, "Arctic" seems like the strongest option for caribou and walrus since neither opponent's animals nor the kill word fit that category. Warthog and owl aren't Arctic creatures, and bluebird isn't either.

I could also try "Northern" for the same pair, or explore whether butterfly, caribou, and walrus share something like migration patterns—though walrus doesn't really migrate the way the other two do. I'm settling on "Arctic" for caribou and walrus since both are definitively Arctic animals, and my opponent has no Arctic creatures to confuse it with. I could also consider "Pizza" for pepperoni or "Clothing" for shawl and nightshirt, though the latter only works for two words and I need to verify the opponent's board doesn't have overlapping garments. Looking at the options, "Arctic" seems like the strongest choice for caribou and walrus—it's specific enough to avoid overlap with the opponent's warthog or the distractor's lamb. I also considered "Tundra" as a viable alternative since both animals inhabit those regions, and "Wear" works well for the clothing pair. I'm checking if there's any way to connect three words, but the combinations feel forced—soft materials would be a stretch for kleenex, and grouping animals together risks collision with other animal clues already on the board. Going with "Arctic" as my answer.
